The Lean iff carries the reverse-direction completeness machinery as PERMANENT hypotheses on the iff itself: (PB : PairOfDefinition B), [IsAdicComplete PB.I PB.A₀], (hBplus_le_B₀ : B⁺⊆PB.A₀), (hφ : Continuous φ), (hAB : A⁺≤(B⁺).comap φ). The completeness/IsAdicComplete hypothesis is genuinely required for the ⟸ direction (Wedhorn's 7.46(2) needs B complete — without it the iff is FALSE, so this is binding-rule (b) territory), BUT 8.39(1) as stated is the adic-SPACE iff that does not expose a single pair of definition; the Lean is the affinoid-ring iff restricted to complete B. The divergence is scope/framing: it is the conditional affinoid analogue, not the unconditional adic-space 8.39(1). The added [IsAdicComplete] is faithful-necessary, not a noeth-ring-of-def defect.

The Lean takes (_hf : IsAdicMorphism) but marks it UNUSED (underscore) and instead ASSUMES the conclusion's prerequisites as explicit hypotheses: (hφ_cont : Continuous φ), (hAB : ...), (hφ_analytic : ∀ v, analytic v → analytic(comap φ v)) [= the f(U^a)⊆V^a that 8.40 DERIVES from hf via 8.39(1)], (PB), [IsAdicComplete PB.I PB.A₀], (hBplus). So the load-bearing analytic-preservation conclusion of Prop 8.39(1) is pushed onto the caller as hφ_analytic rather than derived from hf — a parametric reformulation that turns the Corollary's actual content (deriving analytic-preservation from adicness) into an assumption. The docstring concedes these 'would be derivable from hf alone once [8.36/presheaf-morphism/completeness] infrastructure is formalized'. Adds hypotheses the source derives; conclusion (φ adic) is correct but obtained by assuming 8.39(1)'s output. Note: no noetherian-ring-of-def; IsAdicComplete = 'V complete', faithful.

flagged.name: Valuation.cGammaPos
flagged.divergence: Wedhorn 4.13 defines cΓ_v as the CONVEX SUBGROUP of Γ_v GENERATED BY Γ_{v,≥1}∩im(v) (the convex-subgroup closure of those generators). The Lean cGammaPos instead defines the SET {γ=0} ∪ {0<γ : ∃a, 1≤v(a) ∧ (v a)⁻¹≤γ≤v(a)} — i.e. γ lies in the convex hull of a SINGLE pair {v(a),(v a)⁻¹}. This is not closed under the group/convex operations that generate a subgroup (no products of distinct generators), so it is in general strictly smaller than Wedhorn's cΓ_v; the docstring itself admits 'this definition captures the bounded-by-some-v(a)^±1 characterisation' rather than the generated convex subgroup. Conclusion/object differs from the cited definition.

flagged.name: Valuation.IsMicrobial

The Lean operationalizes it as ∀γ>0, ∃a, 1≤v(a)∧(v a)⁻¹≤γ≤v(a) — i.e. cGammaPos=all positives — which inherits the cGammaPos single-pair-hull defect and is not the cited/standard definition. Cite is to an 'implicit' passage that does not state this.

flagged.name: Wedhorn.isClosed_ideal_of_noetherian
flagged.citation: Wedhorn Prop 6.17, ideal form (wedhorn.txt:2449-2452) — "A is noetherian if and only if every ideal is closed"
flagged.divergence: Wedhorn 6.17 hypotheses for the ⟹ direction are: A complete Tate ring AND **A noetherian** (the full ring), giving 'every ideal closed'. The Lean instead assumes **[IsNoetherianRing ↥P.A₀]** — a NOETHERIAN RING OF DEFINITION — not [IsNoetherianRing A]. By Wedhorn Remark 6.37(3) (wedhorn.txt:2687) 'every Tate ring with a noetherian ring of definition is strongly noetherian', and a strongly-noetherian Tate ring IS noetherian, so noetherian-A₀ ⟹ noetherian-A; the substituted hypothesis is therefore STRICTLY STRONGER than 6.17's. This is precisely the case-(a) noetherian-ring-of-definition assumption (ℂ_p has none) substituted for the weaker case-(b)-compatible 'A noetherian'. The docstring itself admits A₀-noetherian is 'automatic for strongly noetherian Tate rings and downstream callers can discharge [IsNoetherianRing P.A₀] from a strongly-noetherian structure' — but that discharge is FALSE for ℂ_p (strongly-noetherian, NO noetherian ring of definition). Faithful 6.17 would take [IsNoetherianRing A] (which IS available for strongly-noetherian Tate rings, the case-(b) setting) and drop the explicit pair P with noetherian A₀.

The lemma's actual content is a reduction of a localized max-element comparison to a comap-image rational-open membership (comap w ∈ rationalOpen T_D s_D), which is plain comap_vle + Finset.mem_image bookkeeping; the '7.45' label is a mis-citation. The genuine Wedhorn driver for the cover-refinement direction is Corollary 7.32 (the dominating-unit construction used in 8.34(ii), wedhorn.txt:4239-4241), not 7.45. The named residual h_base_rational_subset_comap is an honestly-carried hypothesis (permitted), but the Wedhorn-7.45 attribution is incorrect.
